title: [AI5] Configure Pi for Kimmy Custom Provider
summary: Located the TNF model configuration at `~/.tnf/models/models.json` and added a disabled Kimmy/Kimi custom provider placeholder. The entry exposes explicit credential insertion keys without storing secrets: `apiKeyEnv` is `KIMMY_API_KEY` and `baseUrlEnv` is `KIMMY_API_BASE_URL`.

checks.command: node -e "const fs=require('fs'); const p=process.env.HOME+'/.tnf/models/models.json'; const data=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(p,'utf8')); const item=data.find(x=>x.id==='mo-kimmy-custom'); if(!item||item.apiKeyEnv!=='KIMMY_API_KEY'||item.enabled!==false) process.exit(1);"

checks.notes: Pi Redis wrapper is running; provider activation still requires real Kimmy credentials and Pi CLI validation.

next_steps: Set `KIMMY_API_KEY` and `KIMMY_API_BASE_URL` from the active Kimmy subscription.
Enable `mo-kimmy-custom` after a successful Pi provider smoke test.
